snippets.description: Import additions for STAFF-004: branchProductAvailability, products from schema; no new middleware — all 4 routes inherit requireStaff from mount point in index.ts
snippets.content: import { branchProductAvailability, branches, orderItems, orderStatusEnum, orders, products } from '../db/schema/index';

snippets.description: LEFT JOIN branch_product_availability with COALESCE: absent row → isAvailable true. Staff-only view. Customer menu uses INNER JOIN (is_available=true only).
snippets.content: db.select().from(products).leftJoin(branchProductAvailability, and(eq(bpa.branch_id, branchId), eq(bpa.product_id, products.id))).where(eq(products.is_active, true)); isAvailable = row.isAvailable ?? true

snippets.description: UPSERT via onConflictDoUpdate targeting composite unique index (branch_id, product_id). Branch always session-derived — cross-branch writes impossible.
Zod .refine() enforces at least one field; branch always session-derived; re-selects after update for response.
snippets.content: patchBranchSettingsSchema = z.object({ isAcceptingPickup: z.boolean().optional(), estimatedPrepMinutes: z.number().int().min(1).max(120).optional() }).refine(data => data.isAcceptingPickup !== undefined || data.estimatedPrepMinutes !== undefined)

snippets.description: Customer-facing menu uses INNER JOIN with is_available=true. An absent bpa row means product is NOT visible to customers — distinct from staff LEFT JOIN default.
snippets.content: INNER JOIN branch_product_availability WHERE bpa.is_available = true AND bpa.branch_id = branchId

snippets.description: New exported types for STAFF-004. basePrice typed as string (numeric(10,2) decimal — not number) per E4.
snippets.content: export type StaffProduct = { id: string; name: string; categoryId: string; basePrice: string; isAvailable: boolean }; export type StaffBranchSettings = { isAcceptingPickup: boolean; estimatedPrepMinutes: number };
